steps.text: Estimate bounds for systematic uncertainty and make them small compared to the random uncertainty, if possible.
Use 16 percent as an estimate for systematic uncertainties (1 percent for blanks, 5 percent for baseline, and 10 percent for calibration factors).
Apply the Unity Rule (Equation 4-5) to ensure that the total dose (risk) from all sources does not exceed the release criteria.
Apply Equation 8-4 to ensure that the total dose is within the release criteria when residual radioactive material is found in an isolated area of elevated activity.
Include a separate term in Equation 8-4 for each elevated area.
Provide Unity Rule calculations, showing one for acceptance (<1) and one for failure (>1).
Perform site-specific exposure dose/risk calculations using an acceptable exposure pathway model.
Move the final paragraph underneath the bulleted list in Section 4.2.1, into the bulleted list above, with the heading 'Optimize the survey design (DQO Step 7)'.
Incorporate Figures 4.1 and 4.2 into Section 4.3, reverse their order of appearance, and place them in closer proximity to one another.
Ensure there is consistency between the Basic Terms in Section 4.7.1 and the Glossary.
Change the heading for Section 4.8.4.1 to 'Method Uncertainty' and discuss the concepts of method uncertainty and required method uncertainty in detail, including its determination and use.
Cross-reference Section 6.4, Measurement Uncertainty in Chapter 4, Section 4.8.4.1.
Include a discussion in Section 4.8.2 describing the rationale for setting the MDC at less than 50% of the UBGR.
Move Section 4.12 and details regarding the calculation of the various types of DCGLs in Section 4.5.3 to Appendix O.
Add a precautionary statement in Section 4.4 regarding the appropriate use of the Unity Rule.
Merge Sections 4.4 and 4.5 because they both discuss use of the Unity Rule.

Add two figures (as in Figure 6.1) to Chapter 4 to illustrate the relationships in Scenarios A and B, and describe in simple language how the relative shift (width of gray area) depends on the values of σ and the α and β error rates.

Consider moving the material in Section 4.12.3.1 into Chapter 5, where it is also covered, with a higher-level summary of sample size determination being presented in Chapter 4, and reference to the appropriate sections in Chapter 5.

preconditions.text: No other information on systematic uncertainty is available.
More than one radionuclide is present and distinguishable from background and a single concentration does not apply, or measurements of different quantities are made at a location.
Residual radioactive material is found in an isolated area of elevated activity in addition to residual radioactive material distributed relatively uniformly across the survey unit.
There is more than one elevated area.
Coordinate with applicable federal or state regulators to obtain guidance.
Actual radioactive material characterization data are available.

constraints.text: Use beta scanning rather than alpha scanning of a porous concrete surface.
The sum of the ratios of concentration to DCGL for each component must be less than or equal to 1 (Equation 4-5).
The sum of the uniform and elevated area terms must be less than or equal to 1 (Equation 8-4).
Using the Unity Rule without regulatory guidance can cause unnecessary effort and expense.
